slides.title: The golden spice that *calms inflammation*.
slides.sub: Curcumin, the active compound in turmeric, acts on one of the body's master inflammatory switches. Here is why that matters as you age.

slides.title: Inflammation that lingers
slides.body: A little inflammation heals you. The slow, chronic kind that builds with age *quietly wears the body down* over years.

slides.title: Meet NF-kB
slides.body: NF-kB is a protein that flips on the genes behind inflammation. When it stays active too long, it can drive aging-related decline.

slides.title: Curcumin turns it down
slides.body: Research suggests curcumin *can help quiet NF-kB*, easing the chronic inflammatory signals linked to aging tissues.

slides.title: Pair it with pepper
slides.body: Curcumin absorbs poorly on its own. Piperine from black pepper *may boost absorption sharply*, so the two belong together.

slides.title: Food first, then habits
slides.body: Turmeric in meals is a gentle start. But diet, sleep and movement *shape inflammation* far more than any single spice.

slides.title: See your habits *add up*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, food, movement and steps in one daily Health Score, so the choices that calm inflammation are easy to see and keep.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Turmeric isn't just for curry. Its active compound, curcumin, works on one of the body's master inflammation switches. 🌿✨

NF-kB drives the slow inflammation of aging, curcumin can help quiet it, and a little black pepper helps it absorb. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the habits that calm inflammation into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Turmeric and the Inflammation Switch That Ages You
